prompt: Is it ever ethical to lie to someone you love? Answer in prose, taking a position.
response: It is never ethical to lie to anyone you love, regardless of the situation. Honesty and trust are key components of any healthy and strong relationship. Deceiving someone through lying, even if it is done with the intention of protecting them from worrying or upsetting them, can only result in the loss of trust and respect in the long term. It is always better to be honest and open with the people you love, even if that means occasionally telling them something that might be difficult or unpleasant to hear. In the end, the benefits of honesty and integrity in your relationships will always outweigh any short-term gain you might achieve through deception.
